message.content: Automated Collection involves adversaries using automated methods to gather internal data from compromised systems or networks. This can include using command and scripting interpreters to search and copy files based on specific criteria, such as file type or location, at set intervals. In cloud environments, adversaries may leverage cloud APIs, command line interfaces, or ETL services for data collection. This technique often integrates with other methods like File and Directory Discovery and Lateral Tool Transfer to locate and move files, and Cloud Service Dashboard and Cloud Storage Object Discovery to identify cloud resources. The impact of this technique is significant, as it enables large-scale data theft with minimal manual intervention. Common exploitation methods include using batch scripts, custom DLLs, and remote access tools to automate data collection. Mitigation strategies include implementing strict access controls, monitoring for unusual file access patterns, using endpoint detection and response (EDR) tools to detect automated scripts, and employing data loss prevention (DLP) solutions to prevent unauthorized data exfiltration. Regular audits and anomaly detection can also help identify and mitigate automated collection activities.
